tldr: Sonner is an open-source React toast/notification library, not an email sending service — this is not a like-for-like replacement for Amazon SES, only an option if you're replacing user-facing in-app notifications.
why_switch: Sonner runs entirely client-side as a React component, with no per-message cost
Useful only if your SES use case was actually surfacing in-app notifications, not delivering email
Removes a vendor and an API dependency for purely UI notifications
what_to_export: Inventory of every code path currently calling SES
Classification of each path: must-deliver email vs in-app UI message
Email templates that were really just UI copy
Suppression list and bounce history (still needed if any email use case remains)
DKIM/SPF/DMARC DNS records (keep until migration is fully scoped)
Any analytics or open/click tracking tied to email
export_method: Audit your codebase for SES SDK calls and group them by intent. For anything that is genuinely transactional or marketing email, plan a separate migration to a real email provider — Sonner cannot send email. For flows that are really just in-app status messages, capture the message content and triggers so they can be re-implemented as toast notifications. Consult the Amazon SES documentation for exact export commands.
import_method: Install Sonner in your React app per the official Sonner documentation, mount its toast container at the app root, and replace the relevant SES calls with client-side toast invocations. Keep a real email provider (or SES itself) for any flow that must reach the user's inbox.

concept_mapping.note: Only valid if the message was UI feedback, not delivered mail
Re-author as React; no HTML email rendering
No delivery, so no delivery events
Position, duration, theming live in the React component

effort: Varies — hours for true UI-only flows, weeks if you'd misclassified email as 'notification'
gotchas: Sonner does not send email, SMS, or voice — do not use it as a drop-in for transactional mail
Anything legally or operationally required (receipts, password resets, alerts) must keep using a real messaging provider
Toast notifications only reach users who are currently active in the app
Audit carefully before deleting SES identities — you likely still need them
No delivery, open, or click analytics from a client-side toast
Confirm scope with stakeholders before scheduling this migration

email.subject: Re-scoping the Amazon SES → Sonner migration
email.body:
Hi team,

Quick heads-up: Sonner is a React toast library, not an email sending service, so it can't replace Amazon SES one-for-one. Where it is appropriate is for in-app UI notifications that we may have been pushing through SES by mistake.

Proposed plan: audit every SES call site, split them into 'must deliver email' vs 'in-app UI message', migrate only the UI subset to Sonner, and keep a real email provider for the rest.

I'll share the audit results before we change any code.

Thanks
